paperTitle: Emotional Tissue: A Novel UI Widget for Character Emotion Representation
centralContribution: This work introduces Emotional Tissue, a novel UI widget designed to represent characters' emotional states in digital games, especially empathy games, as an organic, pulsating shaded cloth deformation. It directly correlates pulse frequency with emotion intensity and customizable colors with specific feelings defined by game designers, offering a flexible API for integration within the Unity Engine.

units.title: Emotional Tissue Widget
units.summary: A UI widget for emotional representation via pulsating cloth deformation in g...

content.description: This work introduces Emotional Tissue, a novel UI widget designed to represent characters' emotional states in digital games, especially empathy games, as an organic, pulsating shaded cloth deformation. It directly correlates pulse frequency with emotion intensity and customizable colors with specific feelings defined by game designers, offering a flexible API for integration within the Unity Engine.

units.whyThisUnit: This is the paper's core innovation: a unique method for visualizing character emotions organically.

units.summary: Core architecture of Emotional Tissue, detailing structural components and in...

content.caption: Emotional Tissue core architecture diagram.
content.description: The system's core architecture, involving `ClothController` and `EmotionalTissueControl`, packaged as a Unity prefab which outlines the structural components and their interactions.

units.whyThisUnit: Understanding the architecture is crucial to grasp how the Emotional Tissue widget is implemented and integrated.

units.title: Emotion Problems & API
units.summary: Challenges in emotion display for games and the flexible API provided for int...

content.description: The paper addresses the challenge that emotional states are often neglected and complex to visualize in graphical user interfaces, particularly in empathy games. The Emotional Tissue Control API, via the `EmotionalTissueControl` component, offers methods like `InitColors`, `Add`, `GetColor`, `SetColor`, `GetValue`, and `SetValue` for programmatic control by game designers/developers.

units.whyThisUnit: This unit sets the context for the problem Emotional Tissue solves and how developers can interact with it.
These figures provide concrete visual examples of the widget's appearance, configuration, and practical use cases in scenes.
